AGENTS.md 128 lines -> 11 + the two bd-managed blocks. It stated the bd quick reference three times and said nothing about Lean. Now a pointer to CLAUDE.md. Its one unique paragraph (non-interactive shell flags: aliases may add -i and hang the agent) migrated to CLAUDE.md's essentials.

THE FOUR DOCS THAT WERE ACTIVELY WRONG (not merely stale):
1. README.md advertised a REFUTED theorem — 'So Ch(K) and V are homotopy equivalent!' — while LabelSpace.lean:237 says that step 'fails for branching K' and Testing/TwoSquares.lean is the machine-checked counterexample. (User has since rewritten the README themselves.)
2. CLAUDE.md contradicted itself 30 lines apart: :14 'MEMORY.md is the live status board, keep it updated' vs :44 'do NOT use MEMORY.md files'.
3. Schedule/DESIGN.md asserted lemma L7 at :289 having REFUTED it at :262.
4. Salvetti/BRAID.md's headline object G K := ⨆ ConcGrpd(K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ does not exist in the code (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). Superseded by BraidFunctor.lean's ConcCatN, which grades by event count in ONE K. SUPERSEDED banner added; the rebase-or-retire call is Cubical-w7x, left open because it is a MATH decision tied to the paper.
Plus: a memory auto-loaded every session claimed the repo was sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean' — that folder does not exist and there are zero sorries.

STRUCTURAL FIX: tracked files pointed into ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, a machine-local dir that is NOT in git — including a LEAN SOURCE FILE (Chains/Segal.lean:817, 'See [[cubechains-segal]]'). Anyone cloning the repo got dangling pointers into one laptop. All removed.

DIAGNOSIS of why this happened (the real deliverable): the junk prose is DEFENSIVE, not explanatory — 'sorry-free' x10, 'unconditional' x7 in one file, 'assumption-free' x7 in another. All true, all worthless to a reader, all addressed to the requester. It is a status report to a grader, misfiled into a source file. Prior instruction attempts failed because they were PROHIBITIONS WITHOUT DESTINATIONS: 'don't write process prose' lost to 'leave the next agent enough context', which had a concrete affordance (a markdown file) while the prohibition had none. Beads supplies the missing destination.

THE RULE, now in CLAUDE.md: if a fact would become false when a bead closes, it belongs in the bead, not in a file. Plus three MECHANICAL tests (would it become false on close? does it have a tense? does it read as an argument that your work is good?), budgets not adjectives (<=1 line of intent per declaration, <=10 per module docstring), and a destinations table. Key correction discovered mid-work: bd remember is NOT git-portable (issues.jsonl exports only _type:issue; the Dolt DB is gitignored; a clone fetches refs/heads/* only) — so durable knowledge that must survive a clone goes in a PINNED BEAD or in git, and CLAUDE.md stays the constitution because it is the only thing that loads when bd is absent.

Remaining work is tracked under Cubical-ndf. Open: w7x (BRAID.md, needs the user's math call).

AUDIT DONE. Two headline findings.

1. UNCONDITIONALITY: CONFIRMED, by Lean rather than by reading imports.
   hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure : ∀ (K : BPSet), HasGlobalEventNaming K ↔ BraidPure K
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Same for braidFunctor.
   Landmine #1 (equivWedgeCat silently carrying NSL/AdmitsAltitude) CANNOT be present: a
   hypothesis-free statement that compiles sorry-free with no extra axioms has nowhere to hide a
   side condition. Importing a file that merely CONTAINS conditional theorems is harmless. The
   import cone is therefore irrelevant to unconditionality — it only matters for the file surgery.

2. THE §8.1 CLAIM IS WRONG. Cone = 58 of 124 files: Salvetti 18, Chains 12, SCHEDULE 11,
   Arrangements 10, Foundations 7. §8.1 says the braid result needs THREE Schedule files
   (EventNaming, EventMapBij, Orientation). It actually pulls in ELEVEN — including Space, Cover,
   Cone, ChainCone, Atlas, HDA, EventLocalSystem, and LabelSpace. The 'schedule space, atlas, cones,
   charts' that §8.1 tells you to quarantine are INSIDE the cone. Only 5 of 16 Schedule files
   (COMSheaf, Horizon, LabelChart, LocalCOM, Sculpture) are quarantinable as things stand.

BUT all 11 enter through exactly THREE ACCIDENTAL EDGES, each thin:
  a) Salvetti/BraidFunctor -> Schedule/Orientation, for TWO names: orSign, eventEquiv_id.
     This single import drags in Cover -> LabelSpace and the whole schedule-space stack.
     orSign/eventEquiv_id are EVENT-layer facts; they sit in Orientation.lean only because that is
     where w₁ happened to be developed.
  b) Salvetti/Normalize -> Schedule/Atlas, for SEVEN names: bslice, pbead, psz, psz_pos, pcubes,
     pchain, prefine. Verified: Atlas lines ~640-940 are pure CHAIN plumbing with zero references to
     Sched/Chart/Stratum/schedCone. They are misfiled — they belong in Chains/.
  c) Schedule/EventMapBij -> Schedule/Cone, for schedCone + 3 friends, used ONLY in the trailing
     corollaries (lines 375-386), AFTER eventMap_bijective (361) and eventMap_surjective (368). The
     bijectivity proof itself does not touch Cone.

ROOT CAUSE, and the thing §8.1 missed: eventEquiv is DEFINED IN Schedule/Space.lean:52 —
  eventEquiv f := Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f)
An event-layer definition parked inside the schedule-SPACE module. That is why Space (and hence the
atlas/cone/chart stack) is in the cone at all. §5 is right that eventMap_bijective is load-bearing;
it is just in the wrong folder.

THE FIX — an Events/ layer, which makes ALL of Schedule/ quarantinable (11 -> 0):
  Events/EventNaming.lean        (move; already clean — imports only Salvetti.Lines + Foundations.Altitude)
  Events/EventLocalSystem.lean   (move; already clean)
  Events/EventMapBij.lean        (move; leave the 4 cone corollaries behind in Schedule/)
  Events/EventEquiv.lean         (EXTRACT eventEquiv + eventObjLinearOrder + card_eventObj_eq_of_hom from Space.lean)
  Events/OrdSign.lean            (EXTRACT ordSign/ordPerm/ordSign_trans/orSign/eventEquiv_id from Orientation.lean)
  Chains/ChainPartition.lean     (EXTRACT the ~290-line p*/bslice block from Atlas.lean)
Then Salvetti/ imports only Events/, and the schedule space, atlas, cones, charts, LabelSpace,
Orientation's w₁, COMSheaf, Horizon, Sculpture, LabelChart, LocalCOM all quarantine cleanly.

ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED AND FIXED: eventEquiv was defined in Schedule/Space.lean:52 as
Equiv.ofBijective (eventMap f) (eventMap_bijective f) — an event-layer definition parked inside the
schedule-SPACE module. That one misfiling is why Space/Atlas/Cone/LabelSpace were in the braid cone.

THREE THINGS THE PLAN DID NOT ANTICIPATE (all forced, all resolved):
1. Normalize also needed the Fintype (EventObj a) INSTANCE, which it was getting through Atlas's import
   chain — invisible to a name-based analysis. Moved to EventNaming next to EventObj.
2. eventEquiv_comp lived in Schedule/Cover.lean:86, and orSign_comp needs it. It went to Events/EventMapBij
   with eventEquiv (putting it in OrdSign would have forced Cover to import OrdSign).
3. Orientable + intUnits_inv_self are used by Salvetti/BraidCharacters (evPerm_sign_coboundary_of_orientable).
   So Orientable had to move to Events/OrdSign; leaving it behind would have kept the edge alive.
   Schedule/Orientation keeps exactly orChar + orientable_of_hasGlobalEventNaming + orientable_of_labelling
   — the w1 story — and its Schedule/Cover import turned out to be DEAD and was dropped.

REMAINING EDGE: Salvetti/SalLocal.lean -> Schedule/LocalCOM. SalLocal is OFF the braid cone (PurityHDA
never reaches it), so it quarantines WITH Schedule/ rather than blocking it. See the note on Cubical-217.

DECISION: OPTION B — repoint is the idiom. Endpoints are a PARAMETER, not a commitment.

The evidence is decisive and it was already sitting in the repo:
- BPSet = <toPsh, init, final>. repoint K u v swaps only the two vertices; toPsh is untouched, so
  (K.repoint u v).cells n = K.cells n by rfl, and K.repoint K.init K.final = K by rfl (structure eta).
- Flow/ ALREADY runs entirely on this idiom: flowHom K u v := ConcGrpd (K.repoint u v), plus flowId,
  flowComp, chConcMor_assoc and the unit laws — all proved against Ch (K.repoint u v).

Option A (generalize Ch/Lines/ConcCat to take endpoints) would rebuild by hand exactly what repoint
gives for free, and would break every one of those existing lemmas. It is pure churn. Rejected.

NOTE the §8.2 premise ('BPSet is bipointed and most of the development does not need it') is
MISLEADING. The bipointing is STRUCTURAL, not spurious: Ch K is by definition the chains from K.init
to K.final, so Ch — and everything downstream (Lines, ConcCat, braidFunctor) — genuinely needs a
BPSet. What those files do NOT do is READ the endpoints: BraidFunctor, Lines, ConcGroupoid and
EventNaming contain ZERO references to K.init/K.final. They need endpoints to EXIST, not to be
anything in particular. Parameterizing them (repoint) is therefore the whole and correct answer.

DONE. lake build CubeChains green (2012 jobs).

repoint was misfiled: a 20-line pure-BPSet operation living in Flow/ChainConcat.lean, at the TOP of the stack (it imports Chains.Segal). So nothing below Flow/ could say Ch (K.repoint u v) without pulling in the whole chain layer — the same class of misfiling as eventEquiv in Schedule/Space.lean.

Moved repoint + repoint_toPsh/_init/_final/_self into Foundations/Bipointed.lean, next to the BPSet structure it operates on, and added repoint_repoint (rfl). It is now reachable from every layer, so Fund/CFund can be stated with hom(u,v) = Ch (K.repoint u v) without depending on Flow/.

This unblocks enr-fund (Cubical-j28) and enr-cfund (Cubical-1mg).

NB: dropped the bogus 'blocked by iso-root' edge. I added it when seeding the board on the theory that refactors should land after the isolation, but a 20-line relocation does not need a smaller surface. The graph was wrong, not the work.

NO CHANGE NEEDED — a finding, not a punt.

§8.2 says 'delete the bipointing from everything that does not use it'. There is nothing to delete:
1. The bipointing is STRUCTURAL. Ch K is the chains from K.init to K.final. You cannot remove BPSet from Ch / Lines / ConcCat — it is what makes a chain a chain.
2. The braid stack never READS the endpoints: BraidFunctor.lean, Lines.lean, ConcGroupoid.lean, Events/EventNaming.lean have ZERO references to K.init/K.final. They need endpoints to EXIST, not to be anything particular. repoint parameterizes exactly that, and it now lives in Foundations.
3. The presheaf-level / BPSet-level split ALREADY EXISTS where it should: Chains/WedgeMap has 6 presheaf-level glue0_* cores under 9 BPSet wedge2_* wrappers; Foundations/Altitude states NonSelfLinked/IsAltitude at PrecubicalSet level and wraps them for BPSet.

The de-bipointing worth doing was Cubical-2g7 (make endpoints a parameter, low in the stack). It is done. Stripping further would be inventing churn.

All three children resolved. Decision: repoint is the idiom (endpoints are a parameter, not a commitment). Execution: repoint moved from Flow/ChainConcat to Foundations/Bipointed, reachable from every layer. Stripping: nothing to strip — the bipointing is structural, and the presheaf/BPSet split already exists where it belongs.

The epic's own title ('most of the development does not need it') turned out to be wrong, and that is the useful output: the development DOES need bipointing (Ch K is by definition chains init->final); what it does not need is the endpoints to be FIXED. That distinction is the whole ticket.
Built and building clean; axiom-clean (propext/choice/Quot.sound only). Braid n, permLen, germRels, ofPerm, ofPerm_mul, ofPerm_one, permHom, permHom_surjective, PureBraid.

Braid/{BlockPerm,Jux,Category}.lean all build clean. blockPerm extracted from Salvetti/BraidDeloop (no proof changes); permLen_blockPerm proved; juxHom via MonoidHom.noncommCoprod; Braids = Sigma n, SingleObj (Braid n) with Groupoid instance; braidsTensor + braidsTensor_map (composition |-> juxtaposition).

Braid/Functor.lean builds, axiom-clean. invCount_eq_permLen (bridge), permLen_evPerm_comp (THE germ relation, derived from writhe being a functor + writhe_braidPsi), braidPsiGerm : ConcCatN K n => BraidFib n (f |-> ofPerm (evPerm f)), braidPhi := FreeGroupoid.lift, permHom_braidPhi (Phi lifts the event monodromy). Per-stratum; global grading split out.

chambersConcat assoc + BOTH units proved, first try, axiom-clean. Key move: state it INDEX-WISE (chambersConcat_assoc_val), where the recursion on A goes through with no HEq fighting; heq_of_chambers then substs the list equality away. Left unit is definitional (rfl). The predicted landmine #2 (erw against serialWedge type args) never fired. (Forced: the blocking bead 9e5 is decided -- Option A -- but is a user-closes bead.)
Closed

braidGrading : ConcCat K ⥤ Braids and braidGrpd : ConcGrpd K ⥤ Braids landed in Braid/Grading.lean, sorry-free. obj x = strands (nEvents x); map f = braidHom (ofPerm (evPerm' f)) ≫ eqToHom recast. Verified by rfl.
